business.headline: Don't click 'submit' yet! These 3 last-minute ITR checks are a mustFiling your ITR before the deadline is important, but filing it correctly is even more important. These three last-minute checks can help you avoid costly errors.

Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey joins Jantar Mantar protestAlakh Pandey joined the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar and urged the government to speak with students. His intervention came after criticism over his silence and widened the row beyond NEET to education and recruitment.

Uber layoffs: Company cuts 10% of customer service jobs as AI adoption growsUber has cut 10% of jobs in its customer service division and asked some remote staff to relocate to office hubs. The move marks the company's clearest link yet between AI adoption, organisational simplification and workforce changes.

Adani seeks rule change to launch airline, challenge IndiGo-Air India duopolyAdani Group has asked the government to relax a rule that limits airport operators' stakes in scheduled airlines. The proposal could open the door to a new carrier even as officials weigh safeguards and existing airlines raise conflict concerns.

Sensex, Nifty extend losses as Middle East tensions push oil past $95The BSE Sensex fell 314.62 points, or 0.41%, to 76,440.43, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 82.50 points, or 0.34%, to 23,913.75 around 9:30 am.

Google posts blockbuster quarter but Wall Street is worried about the billAlphabet reported a strong June quarter led by record Google Cloud growth and higher advertising revenue. Even so, investors focused on its bigger AI spending plan and the delayed Gemini rollout.

Why Tier-II and Tier-III cities are driving India's next consumption boomFor years, India's consumer story centred on the metros. Today, smaller cities are rapidly taking the lead in spending, aspirations and premium consumption. Why are Tier-II and Tier-III cities booming?

Govt rules out using unclaimed LIC, EPFO funds for other purposesThe government told the Lok Sabha there is no proposal to divert unclaimed LIC funds or EPFO inoperative account money. It said the existing transfer and settlement mechanisms will continue while outreach to claimants is stepped up.

US-India trade deal likely in 3-4 months, says senior US officialA senior US official has said that negotiations between the two countries are almost complete, with only a few procedural steps remaining before the deal can be finalised.

Middle East conflict could drag global growth to 1.3% in 2026, warns World BankAccording to World Bank Chief Economist Indermit Gill, if hostilities continue for several more months, the world could face slower growth, higher inflation and rising borrowing costs.

Sensex ends 715 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IndiGo down 4%The BSE Sensex fell 715.06 points, or 0.92%, to close at 76,755.05, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 191.45 points, or 0.79%, to settle at 23,996.25, slipping below the key 24,000 mark.
Can you settle pending PF damages at lower rates? This EPFO scheme can helpOne of the biggest attractions of the Vishwas 2026 Scheme is the reduced rate of penal damages.

India may open skies to new airlines in bid to challenge IndiGo-Air India duopolyIndia is considering easing rules to let airport operators such as Adani and GMR run airlines. The move is aimed at widening competition in a domestic market dominated by IndiGo and Air India.

Property tax rules, AIS errors and more: 6 biggest ITR filing challenges this yearITR filing may be digital and largely pre-filled, but taxpayers are still running into several roadblocks. I spoke to tax experts to find out where taxpayers are making the most mistakes this filing season. Are you making one of them too?

Why are your foreign bank accounts and investments showing in AIS?Did you log into your Annual Information Statement (AIS) and notice your foreign bank account or overseas investments listed there? Wondering how the Income Tax Department got those details and what it means for your ITR filing?

Lupin, Aurobindo, Sun Pharma fall up to 4%: Why are pharma stocks falling today?Dalal Street fell sharply after Donald Trump announced a phased tariff plan for imported generic medicines. The proposal has raised concerns over costs, manufacturing shifts and the long-term outlook for Indian drugmakers.

Sensex tanks 700 points: 3 reasons why the stock market is falling todayBenchmark indices extended their losses in afternoon trade, with the BSE Sensex tumbling 746.20 points, or 0.96%, to 76,723.91, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 198.55 points, or 0.82%, to slip below the key 24,000 mark and trade at 23,989.15 around 12:24 pm.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Brent crude climbs above $92The BSE Sensex fell 367.92 points, or 0.47%, to 77,102.19, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 99.10 points, or 0.41%, to 24,088.60 at around 9:20 am.

Stock market today: Will Sensex, Nifty fall as crude crosses $92 mark?GIFT Nifty futures were trading around 24,117 at 7:56 am IST, indicating that the Nifty50 could open below Tuesday's close of 24,187.70.

Are you eligible for additional UPS benefits? Here's what govt saysReplying to a question in the Lok Sabha, Minister of State for Finance Pankaj Chaudhary said that 25,756 retired Central government subscribers are eligible to claim additional benefits under the UPS.

entertainment.headline: Naseeruddin Shah says celebs silent on students' protest are like 'dogs with bone' Naseeruddin Shah has backed students protesting at Jantar Mantar and questioned Bollywood's silence. His remarks came as the agitation over exam irregularities and alleged police action drew wider film industry support.

For the students. By the students: Alia Bhatt lends support Alia Bhatt has voiced support for students protesting at Jantar Mantar over the NEET paper leak and education reforms. Her message adds to growing film industry backing as students seek accountability and wider attention to their demands.

Video: Trisha arrives at Chennai theatre to watch Vijay's Jana Nayagan Trisha Krishnan arrived at Rohini Theatre in Chennai to watch Vijay's Jana Nayagan on release day. Her visit added to the attention around the delayed film, which is being billed as Vijay's farewell release.

A Shop for Killers S2 first impression: Lee Dong-wook is back, and so is the chaosRelease Date:Wed Jul 22, 2026 A Shop for Killers returns for its second season with Lee Dong-wook back as Jeong Jin-man and Ji-an facing new threats. The opening episodes keep the action tight and the mystery alive, even as the expanding world tests the emotional core.

Did Vijay charge Rs 220 crore for Jana Nayagan? Cast's reported fees explained Reports claim Thalapathy Vijay earned between Rs 220 crore to Rs 275 crore for Jana Nayagan, potentially making it one of Indian cinema's biggest paydays. Here's a look at the reported salaries of H Vinoth, Anirudh Ravichander, Pooja Hegde, Bobby Deol and the rest of the cast.

Jana Nayagan review: Vijay deserved a better goodbye than this bloated spectacleRelease Date:Thu Jul 23, 2026 Jana Nayagan movie review: Director H Vinoth's film, starring Vijay, Mamitha Baiju and Bobby Deol, wants to be both a commercial entertainer and a TVK manifesto, and ends up doing neither justice.

Jana Nayagan's title card isn't a tribute to CM Vijay. It's a cheap shot at Stalin Jana Nayagan's title card gave Vijay's fans exactly what they wanted: a sitting Chief Minister as the hero. But burying an old, borrowed swipe at Stalin inside it turned a well-earned victory lap into an unnecessary, cheap parting shot.

Jana Nayagan X review: Vijay shines in an average film, say fans Jana Nayagan opened in theatres on Wednesday after a seven-month delay, with early X reviews split on Vijay's final film. The response highlights strong fan interest in his swansong even as viewers debate its story, length and second-half execution.

It wasn't my final cut: Anurag Kashyap says criticism of Bandar was 'quite valid' Anurag Kashyap said on Kunal Kamra's channel that he could not own the released cut of Bandar. He said studio edits changed the film's perspective, making the criticism against it valid.

Imran Khan joins student protest march in Mumbai, clicks selfies. Watch Imran Khan joined a Mumbai protest march backing the CJP-led student agitation after police action in Delhi. The demonstrations gathered wider celebrity support as the Centre moved to tighten security in the capital.

Huma Qureshi, Saqib Saleem visit Jantar Mantar late at night to back CJP protest Actors Huma Qureshi and Saqib Saleem visited Jantar Mantar late at night to show their support for the ongoing student protest against NEET examination irregularities. Their presence, along with backing from other film personalities like Salman Khan, highlights growing solidarity with the nationwide movement demanding transparency and accountability in the examination system.

Is 2026 set to be the biggest year of Yash's career? Actor Yash is set to headline Toxic and Ramayana after his lengthy post-KGF gap. The back-to-back pan-India projects have put his range and box office pull under fresh focus.

Dulquer Salmaan, Pooja Bhatt to Imtiaz Ali: Celebs rally behind CJP protest More film and music personalities have backed students protesting over the alleged NEET paper leak and the crackdown on demonstrators. Their posts have called for empathy, accountability and peaceful dialogue as protests continue.

Paper leak a serious issue, students showed courage: Salman Khan backs CJP protest Salman Khan has expressed his support for the CJP protest, commending students for their peaceful demand for educational reforms while cautioning against politicisation.

Aamir Khan's ex-sister-in-law says actor's brother Hyder Ali Khan has schizophrenia Eva Grover has spoken about her marriage to Hyder Ali Khan, alleging years of physical abuse amid his schizophrenia. She said the relationship ended in divorce, a long custody separation from her daughter and lasting concern over his present condition.

The End of Oak Street trailer: Anne Hathaway's suburb turns dinosaur battleground Warner Bros has released a new trailer and posters for The End of Oak Street, starring Anne Hathaway and Ewan McGregor. The footage stresses the film's core hook as the Platt family battles dinosaurs and tries to stay together after a cosmic event upends their neighbourhood.

Shraddha Kapoor hunts for new lead as Aamir Khan steps out of Ashneer Grover biopic Actor Aamir Khan has reportedly exited the Ashneer Grover biopic after creative differences with Rahul Mody. The film also featured actor-turned-producer Shraddha Kapoor in a key role.

Meet Devyn Dalton, Matt Damon's 34-year-old female stunt double in The Odyssey Matt Damon said his muscular look in The Odyssey did not belong to him alone, crediting stunt performer Devyn Dalton for key shots in the Laestrygonians sequence. 34-year-old Dalton is a Canadian artist with a promising body of work.

Disha Patani's sister Khushboo faces lathi-charge at CJP's Chalo Sansad march Actor Disha Patani's sister Khushboo said she was caught in a lathi-charge during a Delhi protest demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. In a video message, she described the crowd as peaceful and said women felt safe despite the violence.

general news.headline: Nothing more important than youth: PM announces fast-track courts for paper leaksPrime Minister Narendra Modi sent a big message to the youth and students amid raging protests in Delhi demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and a showdown in Parliament.

Surrender in 3 weeks: SC cancels honeymoon murder accused Sonam Raghuvanshi's bailThe Supreme Court cancelled the bail of Sonam Raghuvanshi, the prime accused in the honeymoon murder of her husband Raja Raghuvanshi in Meghalaya, directing her to surrender within three weeks.

I found out why thousands are flooding Jantar Mantar now. It's not Wangchuk or DipkeA protest once confined to a few thousand people has now swelled into a crowd too large to count accurately. Protesters say the July 20 lathi-charge and use of tear gas by Delhi Police and RAF personnel has transformed the frustration of students into anger, triggering a fresh wave of agitation at Jantar Mantar.

Cabin crew member raped in office cab on way to Hyderabad airport, driver arrestedAccording to police, the incident took place in the afternoon of July 20, when the cabin crew member boarded an airline-arranged cab from Hyderabad's Shamshabad to the airport to report for her duty.

Bumper monsoon: 10,000-km-long rain cloud seen from India to South KoreaSatellite imagery has captured a vast monsoon rain band stretching from north India to South Korea. The spell has sharply lifted rainfall across India while prompting flood and landslide warnings in vulnerable regions.

You're the one who harmed youths' future the most: Rahul Gandhi slams PM ModiRahul Gandhi claimed that PM Narendra Modi had allowed and encouraged the total capture and destruction of India's education system, and protected every person responsible for it.

Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey joins Jantar Mantar protestAlakh Pandey joined the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar and urged the government to speak with students. His intervention came after criticism over his silence and widened the row beyond NEET to education and recruitment.

Dropping key demand, is Sonam Wangchuk giving govt an exit from protests?Activist Sonam Wangchuk, whose hunger strike nourished the protest against the NEET-UG paper leak at Jantar Mantar, has given the government an off-ramp. An end to the protests might be in sight with Wangchuk toning down his demand for Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. This, as Rahul Gandhi had just begun.

Adani seeks rule change to launch airline, challenge IndiGo-Air India duopolyAdani Group has asked the government to relax a rule that limits airport operators' stakes in scheduled airlines. The proposal could open the door to a new carrier even as officials weigh safeguards and existing airlines raise conflict concerns.

For the students. By the students: Alia Bhatt lends supportAlia Bhatt has voiced support for students protesting at Jantar Mantar over the NEET paper leak and education reforms. Her message adds to growing film industry backing as students seek accountability and wider attention to their demands.

From Andhra govt school to Moon mission: Carpenter's daughter joins global space teamA Class 10 student from an Andhra Pradesh government school has been selected among India's 20 participants for Mission ShakthiSAT, the world's first all-girls lunar CubeSat mission. Daughter of a carpenter, Kandula Jessie's journey from a school tinkering lab to a global space programme is inspiring students across the country.

I'm a doctor, I use AI every day, but it will never replace my clinical judgementArtificial intelligence is changing the way doctors work, from reducing paperwork to helping them stay updated with the latest medical research. Dr Nikhil Rajvanshi shares how AI has become a trusted assistant in his daily practice and why medicine will always need the human touch.

Khichdi failed: Why Nasa cleared just three space foods prepared by DRDOShubhanshu Shukla said DRDO prepared multiple Indian dishes for his ISS mission, but only three were cleared for flight. The episode highlights the strict testing and time pressures shaping India's space food plans for Gaganyaan.

Video: Trisha arrives at Chennai theatre to watch Vijay's Jana NayaganTrisha Krishnan arrived at Rohini Theatre in Chennai to watch Vijay's Jana Nayagan on release day. Her visit added to the attention around the delayed film, which is being billed as Vijay's farewell release.

Will intervene if Supreme Court metro station is not opened by lunch: Chief JusticeThe Chief Justice said the Supreme Court administration had already been asked to take up the matter with the Delhi Metro authorities and assured the Bar that if the issue remained unresolved by lunchtime, he would personally intervene.

Jana Nayagan's title card isn't a tribute to CM Vijay. It's a cheap shot at StalinJana Nayagan's title card gave Vijay's fans exactly what they wanted: a sitting Chief Minister as the hero. But burying an old, borrowed swipe at Stalin inside it turned a well-earned victory lap into an unnecessary, cheap parting shot.

Live: CJP responds to PM's fast-track courts vow, insists on Pradhan's resignationCJP Protest in Delhi Live Updates: Protesters of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) reclaimed Delhi's Jantar Mantar after police dismantled the protest stage and tents earlier following a day of clashes between demonstrators and security personnel. Heavy deployment of Delhi Police and Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel remained in place around the protest site. Leader of the Opposition Rahul Gandhi, whose absence from the protest had drawn attention as several opposition leaders and prominent citizens thronged the site, later led a group of Congress MPs in a sit-in outside the Prime Minister's residence. This led to dramatic scenes as police removed Gandhi and other lawmakers from the area. Gandhi was briefly detained before being released. Tensions escalated again late Tuesday when stone-pelting was reported on Delhi Police and RAF personnel in Connaught Place, a short distance from Jantar Mantar. Stay with Indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

French heatwave toll crosses 20,000, funeral homes overwhelmedNew figures emerging from Europe reveal the scale of a climate disaster that unfolded over just a few weeks, painting a far more sobering picture than early estimates suggested.

Uber layoffs: Company cuts 10% of customer service jobs as AI adoption growsUber has cut 10% of jobs in its customer service division and asked some remote staff to relocate to office hubs. The move marks the company's clearest link yet between AI adoption, organisational simplification and workforce changes.

Don't expect medal rush: Why India could be in for a quiet CWG 2026India has been amongst the medal when it comes to the Commonwealth Games in recent times, but this time the excitement has been watered down. A reduced roster of events and with some historic medal-winning events missing, India could be in for a quiet outing in Glasgow this year.

Godzilla El Nino should mean less monsoon rain, so why is 95% of India cloudy?A Godzilla-strength El Nino should mean a weak monsoon, yet 95 per cent of India is under cloud cover this week. Here is why the monsoon trough, the Bay of Bengal and the Indian Ocean Dipole can still overrule the Pacific.

Dharmendra Pradhan's daughter quits Instagram after foreign degree backlashNaimisha Pradhan deactivated her Instagram account after users targeted her over studying abroad while her father faces criticism over examination issues. The episode has widened the row into a debate over online abuse of relatives who hold no public office.

Delhi Metro shuts 16 key stations as CJP protest swells. Check full listSixteen Delhi Metro stations in central Delhi were shut on Thursday for security reasons as crowds grew at the Cockroach Janata Party's ongoing Jantar Mantar protest. The demonstration, demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over examination paper leaks, entered its third day after the 'Sansad Chalo' march.

sports.headline: Keep faith in government: Dhawan urges patience from students amid protests Shikhar Dhawan asked protesting students to stay patient and trust the government amid the CJP protests in Delhi. The statement comes after the demonstrations over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak and examination irregularities have continued to garner nationwide attention.

Argentina fans launch petition to replay the World Cup 2026 final. Here's why Argentina supporters have launched a petition asking FIFA to replay the 2026 World Cup final after the loss to Spain. The campaign attacks the officiating of referee Slavko Vincic, calling it biased.

Olympic gold medallist Abhinav Bindra backs students amid protests Olympic gold medalist Abhinav Bindra shared a message on the ongoing student protests at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, urging India to strengthen its education system. His remarks come as nationwide demonstrations over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak continue to intensify.

Ramos takes a jibe at Messi by recreating Argentina star's iconic World Cup pic After Spain beat Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final, Sergio Ramos recreated Lionel Messi's viral trophy-sleeping photo to take a light-hearted jibe at the Argentina captain. Messi and Co. were beaten by Spin 1-0 in the showdown in New York.

Rodri set for surgery after Spain's World Cup win as Real Madrid links persist Spain's World Cup-winning captain Rodri is set to undergo back surgery after a stellar tournament that earned him the Golden Ball. The latest injury setback comes as uncertainty continues over his Manchester City future, with contract talks unresolved and Real Madrid links refusing to fade.

Messi's Inter Miami faces MLS investigation over alleged Casemiro deal tampering Inter Miami's signing of Casemiro has quickly come under scrutiny after MLS launched a tampering investigation into the transfer. The probe centres on discovery rights, reviving questions over the club's transfer dealings despite securing another marquee signing alongside Lionel Messi.

Don't expect medal rush: Why India could be in for a quiet CWG 2026 India has been amongst the medal when it comes to the Commonwealth Games in recent times, but this time the excitement has been watered down. A reduced roster of events and with some historic medal-winning events missing, India could be in for a quiet outing in Glasgow this year.

CWG 2026: India's full schedule, list of athletes, complete event list India's Commonwealth Games 2026 campaign begins on July 23 with a 126-member contingent aiming to return with around 30 medals in Glasgow. Here's the complete date-wise schedule featuring Neeraj Chopra, Mirabai Chanu, Lovlina Borgohain, Murali Sreeshankar, Tejaswin Shankar and every major Indian medal contender.

Heartbreak for Sindhu as she loses China Open pre-quarters marathon to Yufei PV Sindhu's unbeaten run has come to an end after she was beaten by World No.4 Chen Yufei in a titanic battle at the China Open on Thursday, July 23.

Shreyas Iyer's desperate India seek T20I course correction in Zimbabwe Zimbabwe vs India: Shreyas Iyer will be eager for his first win as India captain as his young side seeks T20I course correction against a spirited Zimbabwe in the three-match series starting in Harare on Thursday.

Who are India's top medal contenders in Commonwealth Games? India have sent a 126-member strong squad for the Commonwealth Games 2026 in Glasgow. Who are India's favourites to medal in the competition? Let's have a look.

When Novak Djokovic backed Serbia's student protest movementAs student protests gather pace in New Delhi, Novak Djokovic's support for Serbia's student movement has come back into focus. The 24-time Grand Slam champion has stood by Serbian students since nationwide protests began after the deadly Novi Sad railway station tragedy in November 2024.

Alireza Firouzja clinches Chennai Grand Masters title after draw vs ArjunFrench Grandmaster Alireza Firouzja won the fourth edition of the Chennai Grand Masters after drawing with India's Arjun Erigaisi in the seventh and final round on Wednesday.

Shreyas Iyer wants fearless India to bounce back in Zimbabwe after rough UK tourIndia captain Shreyas Iyer wants his players to play fearless cricket and ignore outside criticism after India's disappointing tour of England. He believes the difficult phase has brought the squad closer ahead of the three-match T20I series against Zimbabwe.

Rahmat Shah to lead Afghanistan in Tests and ODIs, Gurbaz becomes vice-captainThe Afghanistan Cricket Board (ACB) has appointed experienced batter Rahmat Shah as the new captain of its Test and One-Day International (ODI) teams following the resignation of Hashmatullah Shahidi.

IPL founder Lalit Modi set to return to India after 16 yearsIPL founder and former chairman Lalit Modi has said he will return to India after 16 years following a tribunal verdict that gave him major relief in the 2009 IPL South Africa Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) case.

Sindhu wildly celebrates win vs Unnati Hooda, shares cryptic social media postChina Open 2026: A huge roar on court, followed by a cryptic message off it. PV Sindhu battled past Unnati Hooda in three games before sharing a social media post that sparked plenty of intrigue.

UTT: Kolkata edge U Mumba in thriller as both teams seal semis spotKolkata Thunder Blades ended defending champions U Mumba TT's winning streak with a dramatic 8-7 victory to secure a place in the UTT Season 7 semi-finals, with both teams advancing to the knockout stage.

Benched in England, Kuldeep Yadav signs for Yorkshire for one-dayers and CountyKuldeep Yadav has joined Yorkshire after being left out of India's ODI XI in England. The stint gives him immediate match time before the Sri Lanka Tests and a county run with the English club later in the year.

Judge us after: Fulton, tactical discipline, and India's quest to exorcise 1975For Craig Fulton, the aim at the World Cup is to end a 51-year-old semi-final curse for the Indian team. With this goal firmly in his mind, the Indian coach has decided to go for a team that will fit into his mantra of tactical discipline, structure and long-term stability.

ICC ODI rankings: Rohit Sharma closes on Virat Kohli as No. 1 battle heats upRohit Sharma gained 14 rating points after his hundred at Lord's on Saturday. Three of the top four in the ICC rankings for men's ODIs are Indians. Shubman Gill is closing on in No. 1 Daryl Mitchell.

Ben Stokes battles cramps, scores first hundred after international retirementBen Stokes returned for Durham and made an unbeaten 108 against Derbyshire despite severe cramps. The innings underlined his resilience and gave Durham a five-wicket win days after his Test retirement call.

Embracing the rollercoaster, Sooryavanshi returns to favourite hunting groundZimbabwe vs India:Vaibhav Sooryavanshi returns to Harare for India's Zimbabwe T20Is after a testing debut in England. The 15-year-old hopes familiar conditions help convert promise into a first senior impact.

Scaloni rejects unfair refereeing chat: Spain were better in World Cup finalLionel Scaloni rejected complaints about the refereeing after Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain. He said Spain were the better side, while admitting that fatigue may have also been a reason behind the loss.

Hasmatullah Shahidi steps down as Afghanistan ODI captain historic 4-year runHashmatullah Shahidi has stepped down as Afghanistan's ODI captain but remains available for selection. His exit closes a notable phase that included a near semi-final World Cup run in 2023 and opens the search for a new skipper before the Ireland series.

David Warner pleads guilty to drink driving, faces up to 9 months in jailDavid Warner pleaded guilty to a mid-range drink-driving charge in Sydney through his lawyer. The conviction could bring jail time and trigger a review of his Sydney Thunder captaincy.

Rohit Sharma proves Rohit Sharma isn't India's ODI problemRohit Sharma answered the only question worth asking of him at Lord's. What India actually need answered are the harder ones: a bowling attack built around one man's fitness, a potential lead spinner being overlooked, and search for all-rounders without a plan.

Did FIFA let Argentina's Leandro Paredes off the hook after World Cup final brawl?Leandro Paredes' red card after the brawl following the World Cup final has been deleted by FIFA. Paredes was seen coming to blows with Eric Garcia and Gavi after the end of the game.

Chelsea take sly dig at Arsenal after completing record Morgan Rogers dealChelsea completed a record British deal to sign Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa after beating Arsenal to him. The Blues decided to a cheeky dig at their London rivals after Rogers picked them over the current Premier League champions.

Think India will bludgeon past Zimbabwe in the T20I series? Think againZIM vs IND: India may be the more experienced side heading into the T20I series, but Zimbabwe can be a dangerous proposition at home and should not be underestimated.

Tilak Varma gives perfect response to strike-rate critics. Here's what he saidTilak Varma pushed back against questions over his reduced strike-rate before the Zimbabwe series. Tilak has struggled to maintain a consistent strike-rate over the last couple of years in T20Is.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
